answer:
The rotational partition function at high temperatures is given by \( q_{\text{rot}} = \frac{T}{\sigma \Theta_{\text{rot}}} \), where \(\Theta_{\text{rot}}\) is the rotational temperature and \(\sigma\) is the symmetry number. This result follows from approximating the sum over rotational states by an integral and applying the Euler-Maclaurin correction.  

\boxed{H}
